PharmD Candidate · Class of 2028

Where science meets compassionate care.

Hi! My name is William and I am a Pharmacy student at the University of Toronto's Leslie Dan Faculty of Pharmacy. My main objective is to become the most well-rounded healthcare provider I can be while also being a fun and active person outside of my professional practice!

Leslie Dan Faculty of Pharmacy
01

About me

I'm a first-year PharmD candidate at the University of Toronto, building on a strong foundation in health sciences from my Bachelor of Science in Honours Kinesiology at the University of Waterloo.

My journey into pharmacy grew from a genuine belief that pharmacists are among the most accessible healthcare professionals. I've carried that belief through every role — from compounding prescriptions and conducting Minor Ailments to delivering over 300+ COVID-19 and influenza vaccinations and organizing patient education clinic days.

Beyond the dispensary, I'm passionate about teaching and mentorship — currently supporting International Pharmacy Graduates as a student lab assistant with the faculty, helping international students build the clinical accuracy and patient interaction skills they need to thrive in Canadian pharmacies.
